title: BEAM Therapeutics BEAM-302 Updated Clinical Data for AATD
text: Beam Therapeutics announced updated safety and efficacy data from the ongoing Phase 1/2 trial of BEAM-302 in alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency. As of February 10, 2026 data cutoff, 29 patients have been treated with BEAM-302 across multiple dose levels (15 mg to 75 mg) and followed for up to 18 months. After a single 60 mg administration, 94% of circulating AAT was correctly folded while the concentration of faulty enzyme dropped 84%, and average circulating level of AAT reached 16.1 µM, exceeding the 11 µM protective threshold. Data from 26 patients support a well-tolerated safety profile up to 75 mg consistent across patient groups, with adverse events being mild to moderate, no serious adverse events, and no dose-limiting toxicities. BEAM-302 is a liver-targeting lipid-nanoparticle formulation designed to directly correct the underlying genetic mutation causing severe alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency. Beam selected 60 mg as optimal biological dose and expects to initiate pivotal cohort in H2 2026, pursuing accelerated FDA approval pathway.
